items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.correct: The ability of different classes to respond to the same method call in their own way.
items.misconception: Having multiple classes with the same name.
items.random_wrong: Variables that change type at runtime.
items.why: Students confuse polymorphism with naming. Polymorphism means 'many forms' - a single interface (method signature) behaving differently depending on the object's actual class.

items.q: What is abstraction in computer science?
items.correct: Removing unnecessary detail to focus on essential features of a problem.
items.misconception: Making something abstract art or vague.
items.random_wrong: Writing code that does not work.
items.why: Students borrow the everyday meaning of 'vague'. In CS, abstraction is a precise technique: ignoring details that don't matter for the current task, while keeping what does.

items.q: What is the index of the first item in a typical array?
items.why: It's 0. “-1” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.correct: A named block that performs actions but returns no value
items.misconception: A block that returns a number
items.random_wrong: A block that takes no parameters
items.why: It's A named block that performs actions but returns no value. “A block that returns a number” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Exact answer: 6. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
